_meta.description: Registration with Revenu Québec for provincial taxes, including GST/QST collection, QPP contributions, and income tax filing obligations for self-employed workers.

plain_language_summary: Revenu Québec is the provincial equivalent of the CRA. As a self-employed person in Quebec, you have specific obligations with Revenu Québec: filing your Quebec income tax return (TP1), contributing to QPP and QPIP, and registering for QST (Quebec's sales tax) if your revenue exceeds $30,000. This is separate from your federal CRA registration.

registrations_required.name: QST (Quebec Sales Tax) Registration
registrations_required.optional_when: Revenue under $30,000 — you can register voluntarily to claim input tax refunds
registrations_required.what_it_means: You collect 9.975% QST from customers and remit it to Revenu Québec. You can also claim refunds on QST you paid on business purchases.
registrations_required.how_to_register: Online at My Account for Businesses (clicSÉQUR access required), by phone at 1-800-567-4692, or in person at a Revenu Québec office
registrations_required.timeline_to_register: Within 30 days of crossing the $30,000 threshold

registrations_required.name: QPP (Quebec Pension Plan) Contributions
registrations_required.mandatory_when: Always — if you earn self-employment income in Quebec
registrations_required.what_it_means: You contribute 12.8% of net business income (between $3,500 and $71,300) to QPP. This is paid annually when you file your return — not registered separately.
registrations_required.how_it_works: Calculated on Schedule Q of your Quebec income tax return (TP1). No separate registration needed.

registrations_required.name: QPIP (Quebec Parental Insurance Plan) Premiums
registrations_required.mandatory_when: Always — if you earn self-employment income in Quebec
registrations_required.what_it_means: You pay 0.878% of net business income (up to $98,000) as QPIP premiums. In return, you're eligible for maternity/paternity/adoption benefits.
registrations_required.how_it_works: Calculated on Schedule Q of your Quebec income tax return (TP1). No separate registration needed.
registrations_required.benefit: Unlike federal EI, QPIP actually provides meaningful parental leave benefits to self-employed workers

registrations_required.name: Quebec Income Tax Return (TP1)
registrations_required.mandatory_when: Always — if you have business income in Quebec
registrations_required.deadline: April 30 for income tax payment; June 15 for return filing if self-employed (but interest accrues from April 30 if you owe)
forms_needed: TP-80-V (Business or Professional Income and Expenses)

who_must_register: All self-employed individuals earning income in Quebec
Anyone crossing the $30,000 revenue threshold for QST
eligibility.voluntary_registration: You can register for QST voluntarily before crossing $30,000 — beneficial if you have significant business purchases with QST (you can claim refunds)

prerequisites.step: Obtain your NEQ number from the REQ
Have your SIN (Social Insurance Number) ready
prerequisites.note: Required for all Revenu Québec registrations

clicsequr_access.what_is_it: clicSÉQUR is Revenu Québec's secure online portal (like CRA My Account but for Quebec)
what_you_can_do: Send secure messages to Revenu Québec

cost.note: Registration with Revenu Québec is free. You only pay taxes/contributions once they are owed.

timeline.qst_registration: 1-2 weeks after applying online; you receive your QST number by mail
timeline.qst_registration_note: You can start collecting QST before receiving your number if you've applied

what_you_need: Expected revenue for the next 12 months
clicSÉQUR access (set up before registering online)
common_mistakes: Not registering for QST within 30 days of crossing $30,000 — penalties apply
Collecting QST without being registered — must refund customers or face penalties
Forgetting that QPP and QPIP are paid when you file your return — not separately — so you must budget for them throughout the year
Confusing QST (Revenu Québec) with GST (CRA) — you register and remit them separately, though often at the same time
Missing the April 30 payment deadline (even if return is due June 15) — interest charges begin May 1

quarterly_installments.how_to_pay: Online via clicSÉQUR or pre-authorized debit
quarterly_installments.note: First-year self-employed often don't pay installments and then face a surprise tax bill in April — budget 25-30% of net income for taxes throughout the year
